channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: British Embassy Hosts Royal Couple, and a Few Hundred More, at Garden Party
item.description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla gathered with Washington’s famous and not so famous for an off-the-record evening.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Julia Demaree Nikhinson
item.media:description: King Charles III and the British ambassador to the United States, Christian Turner, pointing, attended a garden party at the British Embassy in Washington on Monday.

item.title: Investigators in Trump Assassination Attempt Look Through Bluesky for Suspect’s Motive
item.description: Federal authorities are looking into whether Cole Tomas Allen posted on Bluesky as “coldforce,” who wrote and promoted liberal views that did not stand out on the left-leaning platform.
item.dc:creator: Amy Qin and Aric Toler
item.media:description: Federal authorities searched a house connected to Cole Tomas Allen in Torrance, Calif.

item.title: Trump Is Dissatisfied With Iran’s Plan to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The proposal would have set aside questions about what to do with Iran’s nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Ships in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Musandam, Oman, on Monday. Many have been stranded since the start of the war.

item.title: King Charles Will Speak of ‘Reconciliation and Renewal’ During Address to Congress
item.description: The state visit of King Charles III comes at a moment of tension over the war in Iran between President Trump and Prime Minister Keir Starmer.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:description: President Trump and King Charles III will meet privately on Tuesday before the king addresses Congress.

item.title: King Charles Visits Trump’s Washington After Chaos From Press Dinner Shooting
item.description: Presidents use state visits to show off all that is appealing about American culture. But the shooting at the White House correspondents’ dinner has cast a shadow over the visit.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers and Michael D. Shear
item.category: Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Donald Trump and the first lady, Melania Trump, welcoming King Charles III and Queen Camilla to the White House on Monday.

item.title: ICE Warehouse Plan Faces Delay Over Lack of Environmental Reviews
item.description: Officials have argued in court filings that the projects are exempt from federally required assessments, but are scrambling after a judge disagreed.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz
item.media:description: A warehouse in Maryland that ICE planned to turn into a migrant detention center, before a court ruling halted the project.

item.title: Charges Against Suspect in Trump Assassination Attempt Based on Shotgun Shell and a Screed
item.description: The authorities say the California man stormed a black-tie gala on Saturday seeking to kill the president.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett, Zach Montague and Michael Levenson
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, said on Monday that investigators were working to determine if the suspect had any connections to left-wing activists.

item.title: Billionaires Are Dominating California Politics, From Governor’s Race to Wealth Tax Proposal
item.description: Between Sergey Brin and Tom Steyer, ultrawealthy people are trying to guide the state’s direction.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer
item.media:description: Sergey Brin and his girlfriend, Gerelyn Gilbert-Soto, at a White House dinner last year.

item.title: Republicans Push for Trump’s White House Ballroom After Gala Attack
item.description: The attack on a press dinner in Washington, which is being called an attempted assassination of President Trump, has also renewed the fight over reopening the Homeland Security Department.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse and Michael Gold
item.media:description: An aerial view of the construction site for President Trump’s planned ballroom.

item.title: Supreme Court Appears Divided Over Roundup Weedkiller Case
item.description: The case could help determine the future of thousands of lawsuits against the maker of a popular herbicide over claims that it causes cancer.
item.dc:creator: Abbie VanSickle
item.media:description: Bayer, the German conglomerate that acquired the maker of Roundup in 2018, argued that federal rules requiring herbicide safety labeling should bar people from filing state-level claims that accuse companies of failing to warn consumers of dangers.

item.title: President and First Lady Melania Trump Demand ABC Fire Jimmy Kimmel Over ‘Widow’ Joke
item.description: The joke was recorded two days before the White House correspondents’ dinner, where a gunman tried to storm the press gala.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: Melania Trump, the first lady, at a press briefing at the White House on Saturday night following the shooting at the White House Correspondent’s Dinner.

item.title: King Charles and Queen Camilla Arrive in the United States
item.description: The royals will spend four days in the United States, two of them in the capital.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:description: King Charles and Queen Camilla of Britain arrive at Joint Base Andrews near Washington for a state visit to the United States.

item.title: Judge Delays Order to Force Penn to Turn Over List of Jews to Trump Administration
item.description: The Trump administration had said it needed the information for an antisemitism investigation.
item.dc:creator: Alan Blinder
item.media:credit: Hannah Beier for The New York Times
item.media:description: The University of Pennsylvania campus in Philadelphia.

item.title: EEOC Is Prioritizing Job Discrimination Cases That Match Trump’s Agenda
item.description: Field staff at the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission say they are being pressured to bring politically charged cases, even with little evidence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Davis O’Brien
item.media:credit: Alyssa Schukar for The New York Times
item.media:description: Andrea Lucas, chair of the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission, has recast the agency to carry out President Trump’s executive orders, according to employees there.

item.title: As Part of Royal Visit, Trumps Will Show Off the White House Bees
item.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla, both avid beekeeping supporters, will visit the White House-shaped hive on the South Lawn.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:description: Bees fly around a beehive crafted to look like the White House on the South Lawn of the White House earlier this week in Washington.

item.title: Ron DeSantis Aims to Add Four Republican House Seats in Florida Redistricting Push
item.description: The Republican-controlled Legislature is meeting in Tallahassee this week to vote on the map, which would apply for the 2026 midterms if passed.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei and Nick Corasaniti
item.media:description: Gov. Ron DeSantis of Florida proposed a redraw of the state’s congressional districts that could result in as many as four new Republican seats.

item.title: Virginia Court Weighs Legality of New Redistricting Map Approved Last Week
item.description: Oral arguments on Monday morning lasted about an hour. It was not clear how justices would rule.
item.dc:creator: Campbell Robertson
item.media:description: A person votes early in the Virginia redistricting referendum at the Fairfax County Government Center in Fairfax, Va., earlier this month.

item.title: Human Remains Found in Tampa Bay in Search for Missing USF Student
item.description: The remains have not yet been identified, but a former University of South Florida student has been charged in connection with the murders of Nahida Bristy and Zamil Limon.
item.dc:creator: Christine Hauser
item.media:credit: Douglas R. Clifford/Tampa Bay Times, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Investigators in Tampa, Fla., on Friday, after they took into custody a suspect sought in the killings of two missing University of South Florida students.

item.title: Sergey Brin Moves to the Right, With a ‘MAGA Girlfriend’ by His Side
item.description: After once backing liberal causes, the Google co-founder has praised President Trump, donated to Republicans and spent $57 million to try to block a California billionaire tax.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer and Kate Conger
item.media:description: Sergey Brin and his girlfriend, Gerelyn “GG” Soto, at a White House artificial-intelligence dinner last year.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague, Devlin Barrett, Maggie Haberman, Amy Qin and Katie Rogers

item.title: Even Before the Gunfire. Many Guests Bore the Scars of Political Violence
item.description: Gunfire at a press gala on Saturday underscored the ever growing club of political figures whose lives have been upended by violence.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Steve Scalise, Republican of Louisiana, was seen being escorted to a secure room on Saturday. Mr. Scalise was shot at a congressional baseball game practice in 2017.

item.title: Cole Tomas Allen, Correspondents’ Dinner Shooting Suspect, Was Propelled by Outrage, Authorities Say
item.description: A man who has worked as a tutor and graduated from the California Institute of Technology was charged with trying to assassinate the president after an armed attack at the White House correspondents’ dinner.
item.dc:creator: Amy Qin, Chelsia Rose Marcius and Shawn Hubler
item.media:description: Investigators worked outside the California home of a man who is being held in connection to an attack at the White House correspondents’ dinner.

item.title: Beekeeper Gets Jail After a Swarm Is Unleashed During an Eviction
item.description: The woman, who is a beekeeper, was sentenced to six months after being convicted of using the bees as a weapon against deputies during a 2022 eviction.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan
item.media:credit: William DeShazer for The New York Times

item.title: Bernie Sanders Pressures Democrats to Reject Super PAC Support in Primaries
item.description: The independent senator from Vermont says top Democrats should tell candidates they will otherwise lose access to party resources.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: The pressure from Senator Bernie Sanders highlights the tension in Democratic politics over how campaigns are funded.

item.title: What to Watch Next in the Nationwide Redistricting Battle
item.description: All eyes are on Florida and Virginia, where a special session and courtroom proceedings set for this week factor into the battle for the House in the midterms.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:description: People arriving at a polling location last week in Arlington, Va., to vote on a ballot measure on redrawing the state’s congressional map.

item.title: Supreme Court Wrangles With Police Use of Cell Location Data to Find Suspects
item.description: Geofence searches allow law enforcement to find suspects and witnesses by sweeping up location data from cellphone users near crime scenes.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow
item.media:description: A 2019 robbery at the Call Federal Credit Union in Midlothian, Va., led law enforcement officials to turn to a so-called geofence warrant in an effort to find leads.

item.title: Eric Swalwell, Tony Gonzales and Accountability in the Post-Post-#MeToo Era
item.description: What constitutes unacceptable behavior? And how many accusers does it take for an allegation to be believed? Societal standards remain nebulous.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Yee
item.media:credit: Caroline Gutman for The New York Times
item.media:description: Newspapers piling up outside the door of Tony Gonzales, a Texas Republican who resigned from Congress after being accused of having a coercive sexual relationship with a staff member who later killed herself.
